basics.name: Carlos David Ballesteros Torres
basics.label: Self-taught software developer who adapts technologies based on problem requirements
basics.summary: I'm a passionate web developer with experience creating systems that optimize efficiency and process control across different sectors. I specialize in analyzing problems and selecting the best technology to solve them. I work with JavaScript, HTML, CSS (Tailwind), PHP (Laravel), Astro, Vue 3, and other technologies as each project requires.

work.name: Monkits Education
work.summary: I collaborated in the development of a mobile application for an educational robotics company using Flutter for cross-platform development and maintenance of native Android applications. The application was designed to provide a smooth and attractive user experience, allowing students to access a catalog, educational materials, and robot control via Bluetooth. Additionally, I gained experience in electronics and microcontroller programming, which allowed me to contribute to hardware integration in the application, improving interactivity and functionality.

work.name: Real de Plateros
work.summary: I collaborated in the implementation of a Point of Sale (POS) system for a company dedicated to pastry production in Hidalgo. The solution enables sales management, ticket printing from a web page, and inventory and employee administration. It was designed to operate on a local server, ensuring direct communication with printers and continuous system availability without depending on Internet connection. Development was done with Laravel in the backend and Vue 3 in the user interface.

work.name: Marquesitas
work.summary: I developed a management system for a dessert sales venture with role and branch administration. The solution was implemented with Laravel and Vue 3.

work.name: Java Application - Tlatlauquitepec Laboratories
work.summary: I participated in the development of a desktop application for a clinical laboratory company. The solution was designed to operate without Internet connection continuously, developed in Java using NetBeans with SQLite as the database.

work.name: Figra-Gas
work.summary: I developed the website for this company using HTML, CSS (Bootstrap), and vanilla JavaScript. The design was simple but functional, highlighting the company's prices and images with a responsive experience.

work.name: Tlatlauquitepec Laboratories
work.summary: I participated in the development of their website using HTML, CSS (Tailwind CSS), vanilla JavaScript, and the Astro framework. The design conveys a corporate and formal image while respecting the company's visual identity.

work.name: MACE
work.summary: I developed a venture with university colleagues focused on developing websites, web applications, and desktop applications. We primarily used Astro, HTML, CSS (Tailwind), and JavaScript.

work.name: Government Department
work.summary: I developed an efficient system for managing requests with a robust database and intuitive, adaptive user interface. The system was based on: MySQL for the database, MVC pattern (Model-View-Controller), interface development with modern web technologies, and testing with XAMPP.

education.institution: Autonomous University of the State of Hidalgo

projects.name: Portfolio
projects.description: I used Astro, HTML, CSS (Tailwind), and JavaScript to create a portfolio showcasing my skills. Deployed on Netlify as a self-taught project.

projects.name: Connect 4
projects.description: I developed a Connect 4 game using React, hooks (useState), and JavaScript. Learned to manage application state and component lifecycle. Deployed on Netlify.

projects.name: Curriculum Vitae
projects.description: I developed this CV using Astro with TypeScript, HTML, CSS (Tailwind), and JavaScript. Implemented a data-driven architecture with JSON for easy content updates.
